Patch Set 3:

Hi Aurelien,

>I'm wondering if these 2 `pr_info()` add value as this is already called in the oom global handling code.

The idea was to provide little more info that we are indeed calling the OOM handler. other than "Out of Memory: Killed process". 

>But when CONFIG_PROC_FS is NOT defined, this function becomes a no-op. I'm wondering if we should not just wrap the all content under the #ifdef, or even don't defined the callback at all?

Since, obd_memory_sum()/obd_memory_max() rely on /proc to be present it will indeed become a no-op when /proc is not available (maybe just crashed before OOM is triggered and libcfs/obdclass is victim). Does it have any helpful info other that the pr_info() string then? No, it does not not. Only thing IMO happens when pr_info() is outside of #ifdef's is for the same reason to provide little more info that we are calling the registered OOM handler.

Patch Set 3:

My feeling was that sysadmins seeing this message won't be really interested knowing that the handler is called and nothing more happened... I don't think that adds value to them to help fixing their OOM

Patch Set 3:

> (snip).. I don't think that adds value to them to help fixing their OOM

Yes. Very rare, however, in theory where 'proc' is not available it has no value. 
I will put pr_info() inside #ifdef as per your suggestion. This way we will be
be consistent to have all info if proc is available or nothing.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

pr_info() printing starting and exit of function adds no value for debugging. New  patch update removes these pr_info()'s. Rest remains the same.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

This is a good start. Is this called before or after the process is killed for OOM?  

It would be interesting to look into whether it is possible to free Lustre items from cache (eg. inodes, DLM locks, etc.) related to the process?  Presumably if the whole node is going OOM then the VM would already tried to shrink all of the Lustre caches?

Patch Set 6:

>This is a good start. Is this called before or after the process is killed for OOM?  

Andreas, my understanding was that it is _always_ called before.

>It would be interesting to look into whether it is possible to free Lustre items from cache (eg. >inodes, DLM locks, etc.) related to the process?  

I will slowly dig into this. My only immediate concern is once the process (task_struct) is determined to be killed for OOM only option we have is to run oom_handler function. AFter this the determined task_struct is going to get killed even if we manage to free pages as the calculation to determin is not called again after the handler. (I am hoping i articulated my thoughts here) - correction/inputs always welcome?

>Presumably if the whole node is going OOM then >the VM would already tried to shrink all of the Lustre caches?

Yes it will. It will try its _best_ to avoid the system crash.

parents.subject: LU-13911 tests: take osc max_rpcs_in_flight limit into account

commit.subject: LU-13594 obdclass: Add OOM handler for obdclass
commit.message:
LU-13594 obdclass: Add OOM handler for obdclass

This patch adds OOM handler for obdclass. The handler
currently only prints max memory that was used by obdclass
along with current memory being used before attempting
to kill the user process.

Currently, when the handler is kicked in the output under
dmesg would look like:

Output:
~~~~~~~~
...
Mar 21 07:02:02 devbox kernel: obd_memory max: 244859953, obd_memory current: 0
...

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
